title: SELECT, Projection, Expressions, Types, and Result Contracts · SQL Scenario Checkpoint
summary: An operations analyst needs a readable, reproducible ticket extract whose columns carry explicit meaning. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a typed ticket projection query pack. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compose SELECT and FROM clauses that project only required columns from a named relation.
Use relation and column aliases to make provenance and output contracts unambiguous.
Build arithmetic, text, date, and CASE expressions while tracing input and output meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Select From · changed case · Quiz Select From
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Return unresolved high-or-critical tickets plus unowned work from the seeded database. Expose ticket_id, a readable owner label, age bucket 6, and deterministic severity/opened ordering; then test a NULL owner and tied timestamp.

Which action demonstrates Select From without dropping earlier contracts?
